zotero.abstractNote: Causal structure discovery is a fundamental problem in science. While observational data can only identify causal structures up to a Markov equivalence class, interventional data can often identify the full causal graph. In this work, we propose a novel framework for causal structure discovery from interventional data. Our approach leverages the power of neural networks to learn a mapping from interventional data to the underlying causal graph. We demonstrate that our method can recover the causal structure of complex systems from a small number of interventions, and that it is robust to noise and missing data. We also show that our method can be used to discover causal relationships in real-world datasets.
